sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
AY ONLY.
Global 5000/6000 PROHIBITED.
EGPWS and TCAS shall be operable.
PF shall hold a current AHQ.

The following are PROHIBITED (NetJets imposed):
• LOC DME RWY 01.
• RNP RWY 01.
• Night Operations.

The only authorised IFR approach for NetJets use is VOR A followed by Circling with Prescribed tracks for CAT B only.
The preferred approach (weather and traffic permitting) is a VFR APCH using initial guidance from VOR A (CAT B and
Deviation from OM-A 8.3.1 para 1 is approved (use of a VFR approach in preference to an IFR approach).
IFR is the preferred method for departure.

VFR arrivals shall follow the routings contained in this briefing. VFR arrivals from the North, for example using the
Brenner Pass and the Reschen Pass are PROHIBITED.

WIND RESTRICTION:
When flying the Circle-to-land with Prescribed flight tracks procedure (as per Jeppesen 19-10 & 19-12), a 15KT MAX
SURFACE WIND (INCLUDING GUSTS) shall be applied. This wind restriction is a NetJets limitation and it is not
applicable to the VFR approach neither to departure procedures.

CRITICAL MANOEUVRING SPEED:
All aircraft are to ensure weight and flap setting permit compliance with the Max manoeuvring speeds and angle of
bank requirements:

CREW GENERAL QUALIFICATION:
For all operations at LIPB the PF shall hold a current AHQ.

CAUTION:
UNQUALIFIED CREW: Contact the Dispatch Supervisor prior to flight for familiarisation instructions.

• PIC Qualification: by completing the LIPB online test and in-flight familiarisation.
• SIC Qualification: by completing the LIPB online test.
LIPB PIC and LIPB SIC qualifications can be checked on the 'Waypoint' App - 'My Currency'.

The validity of the online test and in-flight familiarisation is 12 months as per OM-A 5.4.1.2.10 Validity.
This crew qualification complies with “1.1 Authorization for operators” requirements (10-1P).
A. PILOT QUALIFICATION TEST INSTRUCTIONS:

PIC and SIC shall register and complete the online course at:
https://ifr-qualification.bolzanoairport.it/login

The qualification takes about 40 minutes. The platform offers the pilot 30 questions out of a total of over
150. The pilot qualification test is passed with at least 80% correct answers.
Once the crew click the link, they will have access to a Pilot Familiarisation Briefing available for download.
The pilot qualification test will be based on the downloaded briefing.
For convenience, this pilot briefing is also available on Comply365:
> 100- «Fleet» Onboard Docs
> 103- «Fleet» CAT-B Aerodrome Briefings >
> LIPB Pilot Familiarisation Briefing

INSTRUCTIONS:

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
IPB is a challenging MOUNTAIN AIRPORT with significant terrain. All approaches require pre-study. LIPB is NOT
modelled in Flight Simulators, so a Familiarisation flight is required prior to operation.
The airport is situated in the northeast of Italy, within the Italian Alps. LIPB is close to the Austrian border and halfway
between Innsbruck (LOWI) and Villafranca (LIPX).

The preferred approach is a  VFR APCH using VOR A to either RWY 01 or RWY 19. This is due to the complexities of
the Circle-to-land with prescribed flight tracks procedures which are flown within Class G (uncontrolled) airspace.

When weather deteriorates below the minima (6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS) for the VFR arrival, the VOR
A is available to Jeppesen minima, with Prescribed Tracks, for CAT B aircraft only.

minima, to position for a VFR approach.

The RNP and LOC DME approaches are PROHIBITED for NetJets as these approaches have not yet been operationally
assessed.

Overview - Looking North.

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
10-10-2P8

-

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
HREAT BASED BRIEFING TOPICS:
CONFUSION
LIPB is an airport with complicated IFR arrival procedures and Prescribed Flight Tracks, some of which vary by aircraft
Category and some which NetJets are not approved for.
NetJets applies more stringent VFR minima than the AIP. Care must be taken to prepare for arrival. Planning and pre-
study is required to ensure you are prepared for a go around or balked landing, particularly the PRESCRIBED TRACKS
following the VOR A. Make effective use of Familiarisation flights to study the terrain and procedures, including the
airport produced Pilot Briefing available at:
https://www.bolzanoairport.it/images/PILOTS_FAMILIARIZATION_BRIEFING.pdf
or
https://www.bolzanoairport.it/en/pilots-corner-en.htm

COLLISION RISK
LIPB is a busy and popular VFR/IFR airport, situated at the head of the main valley throughfare between Italy and
Austria. GA, helicopter, and gliders traffic may be expected, including VFR arrivals from the N.

LIPB has a combined ATZ and RMZ, (Class G GND-6500ft). Only one IFR arrival is allowed at any time, with VFR traffic
required to remain clear of the ATZ during IFR operations. However, LIPB has confirmed that terrain masking,
procedures partially in uncontrolled airspace and the locality of Helicopter sites makes this impractical and warns
that VFR traffic may be encountered at any time during IFR procedures. Crew must adhere to OM-A 8.3.1 VFR Flight
Policy and maximise use of TCAS and lookout during operations to LIPB.
Crew must not expect to be protected during IFR (VOR A) approaches. Significantly, LIPB prefers arrivals to follow the
VFR procedures outlined in this briefing as they are more predictable to most airspace users and reduce flight time in
busy airspace.

CFIT
Significant mountainous terrain exists surrounding the airport. A lack of radar and protected instrument approaches,
combined with protracted VFR arrivals, increases the risk of CFIT. Crew should follow the procedures and
recommended vertical profiles in this briefing. Crew must make a prompt decision to abort any approach if the
weather conditions are below minima. EGPWS is mandatory for operations to LIPB. Crew must also ensure their OEI
performance is adequate for the conditions. Note: The valley to the south is a safe escape route, leading to a large
lake and to the most suitable close airport - Villafranca (LIPX) at 194° / 66nm.

UNSTABLE APPROACHES
The only IFR approach approved for our operation is the VOR A followed by circle-to-land with prescribed tracks for
BOTH RWY 01 and RWY 19. This is only available to CAT B aircraft to the JEPPESEN MINIMA. Both CAT B and CAT C
may fly the initial VOR A to higher minima followed by positioning for a VFR arrival. High terrain makes the VOR A
unsuitable for a visual straight in to RWY 01 from the Missed Approach Point, and RWY 19 requires manoeuvring at
slow speed to land. To mitigate unstable approaches, crew must plan descents and configuration changes carefully.
Guidance is in 9. ARRIVAL PROCEDURES. Be aware that when flying a VFR approach to RWY 19, the airport will be
out of sight during the downwind positioning. Finally, displaced thresholds on both RWYs increase the risk of an
optical illusion during final approach, and PAPIs are steeper than usual and set at 4.48°.

In all situations, crew must be prepared to GO-AROUND from an unstable approach.

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
10-10-2P9

-

VFR procedures outlined in this briefing as they are more predictable to most airspace users and reduce flight time in
Significant mountainous terrain exists surrounding the airport. A lack of radar and protected instrument approaches,
combined with protracted VFR arrivals, increases the risk of CFIT. Crew should follow the procedures and

The only IFR approach approved for our operation is the VOR A followed by circle-to-land with prescribed tracks for

Guidance is in 9. ARRIVAL PROCEDURES. Be aware that when flying a VFR approach to RWY 19, the airport will be

In all situations, crew must be prepared to GO-AROUND from an unstable approach.

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES - GENERAL
The Straight in VFR APCH RWY 01 using VOR A for the initial descent is available to both CAT B and CAT C aircraft.
This is the preferred approach when weather and local traffic permits.

The VOR A is available to both CAT B and CAT C aircraft; however, the procedure terminates at the VOR and requires
either a circle-to-land with prescribed tracks (CAT B only) or a VFR approach with significantly higher minimums and
an earlier break-off point from the VOR A (CAT B or CAT C).

A VFR approach to RWY 19 is approved for both CAT B and C aircraft but requires slow speed manoeuvring.

It is recommended the latest break-off point from the VOR when performing the VFR APCH using VOR A:
• RWY 01: D10 OZE (D13IBZ). This is because the required rate of descent inside this distance becomes excessive
for a successful approach.
• RWY19: D6.5 OZE (D3.5 IBZ). This allows the aircraft to position safely into the valley for the downwind.

During all approaches, FDM has shown there is a possibility of EGPWS alerts caused by restricted lateral separation
and spurs of terrain - see the appropriate arrival section for details.

FACING SOUTH: AIRPORT IS IN CENTRE(-RIGHT) OF IMAGE
NOTE TERRAIN SURROUNDING THE AIRFIELD

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P10

-
9.2. VFR APCH RWY 01 using VOR A
MINIMA:

DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8 km. Ceiling: 8313 ft (9100 ft QNH)
Note: If OZE VOR and or DME is unavailable then Ceiling minima is increased to 11,713
ft (12,500 QNH)

JEPPESEN CHARTS:
13-1, 19-1.

PERFORMANCE:

Use the following setup:
ARRIVAL -  01 and
DEPARTURE: 01DP1. (for Balked Landing scenario).

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following options:
Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 01. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’, and;
Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 01DP1. Input
actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to DRY RWY
regardless of actual RWY state.

PROCEDURE:
Expect IFR STAR to FORER followed by VOR A with a break-off for VFR Approach (straight-
in) to RWY 01.

Weather and performance permitting, this is the preferred approach. Crew should NOT
attempt to arrive VFR from the North by use of the northern valleys.

The VFR approach straight-in to RWY 01 is performed after breaking off from the VOR A
when VMC is assured.

The earliest point to break off-off from the VOR A is FORER (Due to airspace).

The latest recommended break-off point from the VOR is D10 OZE (D13IBZ). This is
because the required rate of descent inside this distance becomes excessive for a
successful approach.

See guidance pictures and recommended profile as per RECOMMENDED VERTICAL
PROFILE for VFR APCH RWY 01 using VOR A

If IMC at FORER, descend as per VOR A to the minima above. Do not attempt a VFR
straight-in approach from the VOR A when inside D10 OZE (too high – unstable
approach). Either perform the missed approach or Visual Pattern.

On maintaining VMC (anticipate between D16-20 OZE or D22-25 IBZ), cancel IFR and fly
a VFR approach to RWY 01 using the South - North Valley. An early transition to the
valley, reduced speed and early configuration changes will assist with descent and
energy management.

CAUTION:
EGPWS alerts may be generated if crew descend VFR following the OZE VOR or IBZ LOC inbound track, or if crew do
not follow the valley contours accurately. All EGPWS alerts must be actioned in accordance with OM-A 8.3.5.

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P11

-
Once VFR inbound, consider use of the LOC DME IBZ to aid situational awareness
especially for the DME.
The VOR/DME OZE is also available and will give a more dependable DME signal at
extended range as it is on a hill, however, be aware that the VOR/DME OZE is situated
3.2 NM before the RWY 01 threshold.

OZE VOR/DME Outage:
If the OZE VOR and or DME is unavailable, then the VFR Approach RWY 01 may still be
flown however the Ceiling requirement shall be increased to 11,713 ft (12500 QNH).
Ensure VMC at FORER, cancel IFR and continue the VFR approach RWY 01 descending
using the IBZ DME in the vertical guidance table.

MISSED APPROACH:
Whilst on VOR A: As per Jeppesen.

BALKED LANDING:
Preferred option is to perform a LEFT-HAND Visual Pattern at Min Alt 3300ft (2500ft
AAL) with Max 155 KIAS at 25° Angle of Bank.

Note this is performed around the spur of high ground – see diagram. The published
pattern height for RWY 01 is 2500 ft AAL – consider higher to increase visibility in the
valley.

Alternatively: Intercept the Missed Approach profile for the VOR A, avoiding terrain and
obstacles visually until established on the R014° outbound OZE. (160KIAS Max until
established on the southerly track inbound VOR OZE). This will require coordination with
ATC and IFR (re)clearance.

CONTINGENCY:
The PDCS 01DP1 take-off procedure may be used for a RWY 01 balked landing scenario.
This provides an IMC procedure with OEI performance to escape SOUTH towards FORER.
Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if departing LIPB).

9.3. VFR APCH RWY 19 using VOR A
MINIMA:

DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8 km. Ceiling: 8313 ft (9100 ft QNH)
Note: If OZE VOR and or DME is unavailable then Ceiling minima is increased to 11,713
ft (12,500 QNH)

JEPPESEN CHARTS:
13-1, 19-1.

PERFORMANCE:

Use the following setup:
ARRIVAL: RWY 19. And,
DEPARTURE: 19DP2. (Balked Landing)

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following options:
Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 19. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’, and;
Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 19DP2. Input
actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to DRY RWY
regardless of actual RWY state.

PROCEDURE:
Expect IFR STAR to FORER followed by VOR A with a break-off for VFR Approach RWY 19.
This is the preferred approach if RWY 19 is required. Crew should NOT attempt to arrive
VFR from the North by use of the northern valleys.

The VFR approach to RWY 19 is performed after breaking off the VOR A & VMC is assured.

The earliest point to break off-off from the VOR A is FORER (Due to airspace).

The latest recommended break-off point from the VOR is D6.5 OZE (D3.5 IBZ). This allows
the aircraft to position safely into the valley for the downwind.

See the following guidance pictures and recommended vertical profile in TABLE 2:
VERTICAL GUIDANCE: RWY 19

If IMC at FORER, descend as per VOR A to the minima above.

On maintaining VMC (anticipate between D16-20 OZE or D22-25 IBZ), cancel IFR and
enter the North-South Valley or continue with the VOR A until no later than D6.5 OZE
(D3.5 IBZ), to break left and make a comfortable approach via a wide Right-Hand visual
pattern.

CAUTION:
EGPWS alerts may be generated if crew attempt to descend VFR by following the OZE VOR or IBZ LOC inbound track,
or if crew do not follow the valley contours accurately. All EGPWS alerts must be actioned in accordance with OM-A
8.3.5.

It is recommended the latest break-off point from the VOR A for the Visual Approach to RWY 19 is D6.5 OZE (D3.5
IBZ). This allows the aircraft to position safely into the valley for the downwind.

PAPI on RWY 19 are set at 4.48° and cannot be used beyond 2.6 NM due to terrain.

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P17

-

OZE VOR/DME Outage:
If the OZE VOR and or DME is unavailable, then the VFR Approach RWY 19 may still be
flown however the Ceiling requirement shall be increased to 11,713 ft (12500 QNH).
Ensure VMC at FORER, cancel IFR and continue the VFR approach RWY 19 descending
using the IBZ DME in the vertical guidance table.

Once established VFR inbound, consider use of the LOC DME IBZ to aid situational
awareness especially for the DME measurement.

The VOR/DME OZE is also available and will give a more reliable DME signal at extended
range as it is on a hill, however, be aware that the VOR/DME OZE is situated 3.2 NM
before the RWY 01 threshold.
At D5.5 OZE, (D9.0 IBZ), turn LEFT and visually fly down the LEFT VALLEY (approximate
heading 350°M). A good visual aiming feature is the lake in the valley at D1.5 OZE.

Continue a 4.0° glidepath, following the valley. The following waypoints may be manually
inserted into the FMS to aid positioning, but they are for SITUATIONAL AWARENESS
ONLY.

LIPB1: (flyby)
N46 21.9   E011 15.6
6450ft QNH
LIPB2: (flyby)
N46 24.9   E011 15.1
4130ft QNH
LIPB3: (flyover)
N46 28.7   E011 15.8
2870ft QNH

Continue following the lateral and vertical guidance shown in the accompanying pictures.
Slower speed (180-160 KIAS) and early configuration changes make the approach more
manageable.

The base turn is made at LIPB3, which is also abeam the distinct ruined Fort on the hilltop
on the RIGHT (RWY is obscured by terrain).

Reduce speed and configure for the base leg and final turn. (Preferably 140 KIAS but
Max. 155 KIAS with 25° Angle of Bank in the turn).

NOTE: Visual contact with the airport will be lost temporarily during the downwind.

MISSED APPROACH:
Whilst on VOR A: As per Jeppesen.

BALKED LANDING:
Preferred option is to perform / continue a RIGHT-HAND Visual Pattern at Min Alt
3300ft (2500ft AAL) with Max 155 KIAS at 25° Angle of Bank.
Note this is performed around the spur of high ground – see diagram. The published
pattern height for RWY 19 is 2000 ft AAL – it is recommended to fly at Min 2500ft AAL to
increase visibility in the valley.
The visual pattern can be used to either position for a second approach to RWY 19 or to
position overhead RWY 19 to climb out to FORER.

BOLZANO, ITALY
BOLZANO
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P18

-

To Climb out to FORER; Refer to INITIAL CLIMB (ALL RWYS) Jepp 10-3B:
• At the RWY 19 Threshold, begin a max. rate climb to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if
departing LIPB).
• Route to OZE under visual conditions, once above 3600 ft QNH and at OZE, climb may
be continued IMC.
• Follow the Jepp 10-3B profile to FORER.
• At FORER, reconfigure for a further approach or join the SID.

CONTINGENCY:
The PDCS 19DP2 take-off procedure may be used for a RWY 19 balked landing scenario.
This provides an IMC procedure with OEI performance to escape SOUTH towards FORER.
Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if departing LIPB).

9.4. VOR A - CIRCLE-TO-LAND WITH PRESCRIBED TRACKS
OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES - GENERAL - Crews must NOT consider they are protected from other traffic when
performing the IFR approach to LIPB, especially when conditions allow VFR flight.

The VOR A - Circle-to-land with prescribed tracks is limited to CAT B aircraft only and should be used when weather
prevents the preferred VFR APCH using VOR A.

When conducting the VOR A to minimums PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS are required. These tracks are complex and
require careful study.

Accurate pre-flight planning must be conducted to ensure that during approach, the aircraft weight and flap setting
during PRESCRIBED TRACKS permit the crew to comply with the max circling speeds and angle of bank requirements.

Note: If weather permits, then the preferred approach is VFR APCH RWY 01 using VOR A or if RWY 19 in use then
VFR APCH RWY 19 using VOR A. However, caution the latest recommended break-off point for the VFR APCH:

LATEST RECOMMENDED BREAK-OFF FOR THE PREFERRED VFR APPROACH:
RWY 01: D10 OZE (D13IBZ). Otherwise,  the required rate of descent becomes excessive for a successful approach.
RWY19:  D6.5 OZE (D3.5 IBZ). This allows the aircraft to position safely into the valley for the downwind.

MINIMA:

DAY ONLY. Visibility: 5 km. Ceiling: 4133 ft (4920 ft QNH)

JEPPESEN CHARTS:
RWY 01; 13-1, 19-10, 19-11.

RWY 19; 13-1, 19-12.

PERFORMANCE:

Use the following setup:
ARRIVAL: RWY in use 01 or 19. And,
DEPARTURE to LIPB, RWY in use 01 -01DP1, or 19 -19DP2.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following options:
Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to RWY in use, 01 or 19. Input
actual landing weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’, and;
Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 01DP1 if RWY
01 in use or 19DP2 if RWY 19 in use. Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-
around configuration. ‘Options’ to DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.

PROCEDURE:

Request the VOR A - Circle to land with prescribed tracks.
Perform VOR A as per Jepp 13-1 then,
RWY 01 in use; Circle-to-land with Prescribed Tracks RWY 01 as per Jepp 19-10, or

RWY 19 in use; Circle-to-land with Prescribed Tracks RWY 19 as per Jepp 19-12.

Ensure performance and configuration allows the Max Circling speeds:
RWY 01
135 Kts
22° Angle of Bank
RWY 19
130 Kts
25° Angle of Bank

MISSED APPROACH:
VOR A:
Jepp 13-1.
CIRCLING WITH PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS TO RWY 01:  Jepp 19-10.
CIRCLING WITH PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS TO RWY 19:  Jepp 19-12.

BOLZANO, ITALY

BALKED LANDING:
RWY 01:
The recommended Balked landing procedure from RWY 01 with marginal weather is to
perform the PDCS 01DP1 from the threshold. This provides an IMC procedure escape
procedure with OEI performance to FORER. Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL
120 if departing LIPB).
Jepp 19-11 has tracks for either a return to RWY 01 or routing to OZE VOR to intercept
the VOR A missed approach procedure as per Jepp 13-1. (See Caution note below)

RWY 19:
Jepp 19-12 has tracks for either a visual pattern for a repeat approach to RWY 19 or
routing to intercept the VOR A missed approach as per Jepp 13-1.
PDCS 19DP2 can be used from Threshold RWY 19 as an IMC escape procedure to FORER.
Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if departing LIPB).

CAUTION:
The Balked landing RWY 01 example procedure as per Jepp 19-11 is complex and difficult to fly. PDCS 01DP1
procedure provides an alternative escape procedure to FORER and can be flown from RWY 01 Threshold.

9.5. VFR PATTERN
A VFR Pattern to the West of LIPB, routing around the hill within the central valley may be used if needed and
conditions permit.
The VFR Pattern provides a method to return for an additional VFR approach following a Balked landing for example
and requires the same heightened minima as the ‘VFR APCH using VOR A’.

MINIMA:

DAY ONLY, Visibility 8km, Ceiling 8313ft (9100ft QNH).

PROCEDURE:

RWY 01: Visual LEFT-HAND Pattern around the central hill.

RWY 19: Visual RIGHT-HAND Pattern around the central hill.

MINIMUM Recommended Pattern height 2500 ft (3300 ft QNH)

Note: The published Traffic Circuit on Jepps 19-3 for RWY 01 / 19 is 2500’ / 2000’. It is
recommended to fly higher to aid visibility and reduce traffic threat.

Due to the required turn radius within the valley, it is recommended to limit the speed
during the VFR Pattern turns to 140 KT; Max speed is 155 KT & 25°AoB.

Do not delay the turn around the Southern side of the hill as the valley starts to
narrow further to the South. Perform the Left-Hand turn to final RWY 01 and Right-
Hand cross wind turn following climb out from RWY 19 at D2.8 OZE / D6.1 IBZ.

The downwind leg of the VFR Pattern uses the same track as ‘VFR APCH RWY 19 using
VOR A’ so the same waypoints may be manually inserted into the FMS to aid
positioning, but they are for SITUATIONAL AWARENESS ONLY.

LIPB1:

N46 21.9  E011 15.6
Min 3300ft QNH
LIPB2:

N46 24.9  E011 15.1
Min 3300ft QNH
LIPB3:

N46 28.7  E011 15.8
Min 3300ft QNH

See VFR Pattern diagram on next page:

This is the preferred approach when weather and local traffic permits.

either a circle-to-land with prescribed tracks (CAT B only) or a VFR approach with significantly higher minimums and
an earlier break-off point from the VOR A (CAT B or CAT C).

and spurs of terrain - see the appropriate arrival section for details.

Note this is performed around the spur of high ground – see diagram. The published
pattern height for RWY 19 is 2000 ft AAL – it is recommended to fly at Min 2500ft AAL to
The visual pattern can be used to either position for a second approach to RWY 19 or to
position overhead RWY 19 to climb out to FORER.
To Climb out to FORER; Refer to INITIAL CLIMB (ALL RWYS) Jepp 10-3B:
At the RWY 19 Threshold, begin a max. rate climb to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if
Route to OZE under visual conditions, once above 3600 ft QNH and at OZE, climb may
Follow the Jepp 10-3B profile to FORER.
At FORER, reconfigure for a further approach or join the SID.
The PDCS 19DP2 take-off procedure may be used for a RWY 19 balked landing scenario.
This provides an IMC procedure with OEI performance to escape SOUTH towards FORER.
Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if departing LIPB).
RECOMMENDED VERTICAL PROFILE FOR VFR APPROACH RWY 19:
Table 2 provides vertical guidance based on a 4.0° glidepath, to achieve 50 ft over threshold RWY 19.
Note: the IBZ LOC DME is located within 0.1NM of the threshold, but the OZE VOR/DME is D3.2 short of the RWY
This table is for situational awareness and guidance in VMC ONLY. It must NOT be used for terrain separation. EGPWS
alert may be generated if crew do not use the valley, (conducting a steep descent using the LOC or VOR).
180 KIAS requires 1275 ft/min rate of descent.
160 KIAS requires 1133 ft/min rate of descent.
150 KIAS requires 1050 ft/min rate of descent.
140 KIAS requires 990 ft/min rate of descent.
TURN TO HEADING 350°M  AT D5.5OZE (D8.5 IBZ).
NOTE VISUAL AIMING FEATURE (LAKE) TOWARDS WAYPOINT LIPB2
OVERHEAD WAYPOINT LIPB2:  NOTE AIRPORT ABOUT TO BECOME HIDDEN BY TERRAIN.
BASE TURN RWY 19 WITH VISUAL FEATURES
9.4. VOR A - CIRCLE-TO-LAND WITH PRESCRIBED TRACKS
OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES - GENERAL - Crews must NOT consider they are protected from other traffic when
performing the IFR approach to LIPB, especially when conditions allow VFR flight.
The VOR A - Circle-to-land with prescribed tracks is limited to CAT B aircraft only and should be used when weather
prevents the preferred VFR APCH using VOR A.
When conducting the VOR A to minimums PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS are required. These tracks are complex and
Accurate pre-flight planning must be conducted to ensure that during approach, the aircraft weight and flap setting
during PRESCRIBED TRACKS permit the crew to comply with the max circling speeds and angle of bank requirements.
Note: If weather permits, then the preferred approach is VFR APCH RWY 01 using VOR A or if RWY 19 in use then
VFR APCH RWY 19 using VOR A. However, caution the latest recommended break-off point for the VFR APCH:
LATEST RECOMMENDED BREAK-OFF FOR THE PREFERRED VFR APPROACH:
RWY 01: D10 OZE (D13IBZ). Otherwise,  the required rate of descent becomes excessive for a successful approach.
RWY19:  D6.5 OZE (D3.5 IBZ). This allows the aircraft to position safely into the valley for the downwind.
DAY ONLY. Visibility: 5 km. Ceiling: 4133 ft (4920 ft QNH)
ARRIVAL: RWY in use 01 or 19. And,
DEPARTURE to LIPB, RWY in use 01 -01DP1, or 19 -19DP2.
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following options:
Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to RWY in use, 01 or 19. Input
actual landing weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’, and;
Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LIPB and ‘Runway’ to 01DP1 if RWY
01 in use or 19DP2 if RWY 19 in use. Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-
around configuration. ‘Options’ to DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.
Request the VOR A - Circle to land with prescribed tracks.
Perform VOR A as per Jepp 13-1 then,
RWY 01 in use; Circle-to-land with Prescribed Tracks RWY 01 as per Jepp 19-10, or
RWY 19 in use; Circle-to-land with Prescribed Tracks RWY 19 as per Jepp 19-12.
Ensure performance and configuration allows the Max Circling speeds:
CIRCLING WITH PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS TO RWY 01:  Jepp 19-10.
CIRCLING WITH PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS TO RWY 19:  Jepp 19-12.
The recommended Balked landing procedure from RWY 01 with marginal weather is to
perform the PDCS 01DP1 from the threshold. This provides an IMC procedure escape
procedure with OEI performance to FORER. Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL
Jepp 19-11 has tracks for either a return to RWY 01 or routing to OZE VOR to intercept
the VOR A missed approach procedure as per Jepp 13-1. (See Caution note below)
Jepp 19-12 has tracks for either a visual pattern for a repeat approach to RWY 19 or
routing to intercept the VOR A missed approach as per Jepp 13-1.
PDCS 19DP2 can be used from Threshold RWY 19 as an IMC escape procedure to FORER.
Climb with Max rate to 12000 ft QNH (or FL 120 if departing LIPB).
The Balked landing RWY 01 example procedure as per Jepp 19-11 is complex and difficult to fly. PDCS 01DP1
procedure provides an alternative escape procedure to FORER and can be flown from RWY 01 Threshold.
3D VIEW: CIRCLE WITH PRESCRIBED FLIGHT TRACKS RWY 19.
A VFR Pattern to the West of LIPB, routing around the hill within the central valley may be used if needed and
The VFR Pattern provides a method to return for an additional VFR approach following a Balked landing for example
and requires the same heightened minima as the ‘VFR APCH using VOR A’.
RWY 01: Visual LEFT-HAND Pattern around the central hill.
RWY 19: Visual RIGHT-HAND Pattern around the central hill.
MINIMUM Recommended Pattern height 2500 ft (3300 ft QNH)
Note: The published Traffic Circuit on Jepps 19-3 for RWY 01 / 19 is 2500’ / 2000’. It is
recommended to fly higher to aid visibility and reduce traffic threat.
Due to the required turn radius within the valley, it is recommended to limit the speed
during the VFR Pattern turns to 140 KT; Max speed is 155 KT & 25°AoB.
Do not delay the turn around the Southern side of the hill as the valley starts to
narrow further to the South. Perform the Left-Hand turn to final RWY 01 and Right-
Hand cross wind turn following climb out from RWY 19 at D2.8 OZE / D6.1 IBZ.
The downwind leg of the VFR Pattern uses the same track as ‘VFR APCH RWY 19 using
VOR A’ so the same waypoints may be manually inserted into the FMS to aid
positioning, but they are for SITUATIONAL AWARENESS ONLY.
See VFR Pattern diagram on next page:

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1 GENERAL
OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES - GENERAL
CAUTION:
The IFR SIDS do not guarantee separation from VFR traffic. Crew should expect a traffic threat; maintain a good
lookout. TCAS mandatory.

Expect IFR departure for CAT B and CAT C aircraft as per Jeppesen. The VFR departure is available with higher minima
and is to be used only as a contingency if IFR is unavailable.
The preferred RWY for departure is RWY 19, this is due to topography and simpler PDCS procedures.

MINIMA:
IFR Departure: DAY ONLY, Visibility 5 km, Ceiling 2700 ft (3487 ft QNH).
VFR Departure (Contingency):DAY Only, Visibility 8KM, Ceiling 11,713 ft (12,500 QNH)

PERFORMANCE
& CONTINGENCY:
RWY 19: As per PDCS ‘19’, ‘19DP2’ or ‘19STPT’
RWY 01: As per PDCS ‘01’, ‘01DP1’ or ‘01STPT’

Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for take-
off.

‘19’ and ‘01’ provide a Straight-Out IMC Procedure which is simplest to fly.

‘19DP2’ and ‘01DP1’ permit increased performance but require specific tracking.
‘19STPT’ and ‘01STPT’ provide a Straight-Out procedure which mandates the use of the
Runway Starter Extension (Start Point) available at LIPB.

CAUTION:
LIPB is performance critical due to terrain. Only the PDCS procedures listed above shall be used for departure.

If the ‘STPT’ PDCS performance is required (WET RWY for example) then the Runway Starter Extension (Start
Point) shall be fully used. This is shown in grey on Jeppesen 10-9 and labelled ‘Start point’ and requires ATC
request.

JEPPESEN CHARTS:
IFR Departure:

10-3B, 10-3C and 10-3E
VFR Departure (Contingency):
19-1, 19-2, 19-3 and 19-3A

Jepp chart 10-3B ‘Initial Climb (All RWYs)’ describes an initial visual climb requirement
for both RWY 19 and RWY 01.

Jepp chart 10-3C ‘Initial Climb (RWY 01)’ describes an initial instrument climb from RWY

The webcam updates every 30 minutes and is situated immediately behind the LOC/DME antenna looking South
along the visual approach path.

- editorial review
